title: OKLM Manifest
description: JSON Schema for an Open Keyboard Layout Model manifest (.oklm.json), draft 0.1. An OKLM manifest must remain readable, correctable and versionable by a human without a proprietary generator: stable field order (follow the order of this schema's properties), explicit names, compact notation for simple mappings. The text core of a manifest is designed to compile to LDML Keyboard 3.0 (UTS #35 Part 7, root element keyboard3); OKLM does not reinvent LDML transforms, reorders, markers or UnicodeSet notation. Design decisions referenced as D1..D30 live in research/Décisions de conception — deep research.md.

schemaVersion.description: Version of the OKLM manifest schema this file targets.

layoutId.description: Stable machine identifier of the layout, lowercase kebab-case.

version.description: Version of the layout itself (not of the schema).

license.description: License of the layout data. SPDX identifier recommended (e.g. EUPL-1.2, CC-BY-4.0).

description.description: Optional free-text description of the layout or of this manifest.

locales.description: Locales this layout is designed for, as BCP 47 language tags (e.g. "fr", "fr-FR"). The first entry is the primary locale.

geometry.description: Target physical geometries, declared as aliases (D5): the physical form factor family (iso-*, ansi-*, jis-*, abnt-*) plus size (e.g. iso-full, iso-tkl, ansi-60, custom). Geometry tells consumers which physical keys exist; omitting it would mean silently missing bindings. Canonical key positions remain ISO/IEC 9995-1 key numbers regardless of geometry (D1/D5/D24). A drawable geometry layer (absolute x/y/w/h, D23-D30) is intentionally NOT part of core v0.1; see the frame-keys / geometry extension namespaces.

levelSelectors.description: Optional declaration of how levels 2..8 are selected, using ISO/IEC 9995 functional qualifier names (D2/D14): modifiers are named by function, never by the physical key that activates them. Defaults when omitted: level 2 = [Level2Shift], level 3 = [Level3Shift], level 4 = [Level2Shift, Level3Shift]. Platform details (AltGr vs Ctrl+Alt, macOS Option) are export concerns.

keys.description: Graphic keys of the alphanumeric section. Key ids must be unique (checked by validators beyond JSON Schema). Frame keys (function row, navigation, numpad, Fn, media) are out of the LDML-aligned core (D11) and belong to the extensions block.

deadKeys.description: Dead-key composition tables, separate from the key mapping (D4). Each dead key is isomorphic to an LDML Keyboard 3.0 marker plus simple transforms: pressing the key outputs the marker \m{id}; each composition entry compiles to one transform rule (\m{id} + base -> output). Dead-key ids referenced from key outputs must be defined here.

conformance.description: Explicit list of normative references this layout claims conformance to (D13). A global "ISO 9995 compliant" claim is invalid; list each part/edition and scope. Partial conformance is allowed. LDML alignment is NOT an ISO conformance claim and is declared separately on the LDML export target.

exports.description: Declared export targets. Export ids must be unique. Target-specific option schemas are intentionally not fixed in v0.1 (exporter research pending); options is a free object.

metadata.description: OKLM envelope metadata (D8): value that LDML does not cover, stripped on LDML export but preserved for non-OS consumers. Pedagogy, AI and accessibility blocks are separable extensions, never core; their inner structure is non-normative in v0.1.

assistant.description: Scoped assistant/AI metadata (non-normative in v0.1). Must not require keylogging or embed predictive models.

extensions.description: Namespaced extension blocks for data outside the LDML-aligned core. Reserved namespaces (contents intentionally unspecified in v0.1): frame-keys (D11: function/navigation/numpad/media/Fn keys, HID pages beyond 0x07), firmware (D10: physical matrix / QMK-ZMK bindings, scope arbitration pending), geometry (D23-D30: drawable absolute geometry), ldml (imported LDML constructs preserved during LDML -> OKLM conversion).

id.description: Canonical physical key identifier: ISO/IEC 9995-1 key number (D1), e.g. D01 (QWERTY Q position), C00 (Caps Lock), B00 (ISO extra key), E01 (digit row). ISO 9995-1 is the de jure position standard used by national layout norms.

hid.description: Mandatory USB HID Usage ID from keyboard usage page 0x07, as a hex string (D1). Unambiguous machine anchor: disambiguates internationally (e.g. ABNT Ro 0x87 is not the ISO B00 key, HID 0x64).

xkb.description: Optional XKB key name alias for Linux export (D1), e.g. AD01, LSGT, SPCE. Note that XKB naming differs from the ISO grid (section prefix, column offsets).

code.description: Optional W3C UI Events KeyboardEvent.code cross-reference (D24), e.g. KeyQ, IntlBackslash, Space.

name.description: Optional human-readable key label for documentation.

levels.description: Outputs by ISO level for group 1 (D2/D3/D14): level 1 = base, 2 = Level2Shift, 3 = Level3Shift, 4 = levels 2+3 combined, 5-8 optional. A level with no output is simply omitted. Compact notation: a plain string is a literal text output (one or more codepoints, covering ligatures).

groups.description: Optional additional ISO groups (D3: groups are complete alternative keymaps, e.g. multi-script; a distinct axis from levels). Group 1 is the levels property above; entries here start at "2".

categories.description: Optional semantic tags (e.g. letter, digit, punctuation, french).

output.description: What a key produces at one (group, level). Compact form: a string is literal text output. Object forms: { "deadKey": id } emits the dead-key marker; { "modifier": name } declares a functional modifier key (D2: named by function, not by physical key).

deadKey.description: Id of a dead key defined in the top-level deadKeys array.

id.description: Dead-key identifier; compiles to the LDML marker \m{id}.

display.description: Optional standalone character shown while the dead key is pending (e.g. "ˆ").

compositions.description: Composition table: base input string -> output string. Each entry compiles to one LDML simple transform (\m{id} + base -> output). Document order is the rule order.

fallback.description: Optional text emitted in place of the pending marker when no composition matches. Cancellation behavior differs across platforms (D4); exporters document their mapping.

reference.description: One normative reference with edition, e.g. "ISO/IEC 9995-1:2026" or "AFNOR NF Z71-300:2019".

scope.description: What part of the layout the claim covers (clauses, sections, key subset...).

target.description: Export target kind, e.g. ldml-keyboard-3, web-tester, xkb, windows-klc, macos-keylayout, keymap-image.

id.description: Optional stable id for this export declaration; must be unique across exports.

options.description: Target-specific options (free-form in v0.1; per-exporter schemas will come with the exporter research prompts). For ldml-keyboard-3, conformsTo must be a whole number of 45 or greater per UTS #35 Part 7.
